title: asphodeline damascena subsp. damascena
intro: Asphodeline damascena subsp. damascena, also known as Damask asphodel, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Asphodelaceae family, characterized by its striking white or yellowish flowers and narrow leaves. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, Damask asphodel is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or ointments, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
